bird.name: Malayan whistling thrush
bird.summary: The Malayan whistling thrush or Malaysian whistling-thrush (Myophonus robinsoni) is a species of bird in the family Muscicapidae. It is end…

relations.subject: Malayan whistling thrush
relations.evidence: Its natural habitat is montane forests
relations.object_summary: 以成熟阔叶林和混交林为主的山地森林系统。

relations.subject: Malayan whistling thrush
relations.evidence: It is endemic to the Malay Peninsula. Due primarily to habitat loss, its population is thought to be in decline.{{efn-ua|"This species qualifies as Vulnerable because development proposals threaten to reduce and fragment its upland forest habitat which would result in a rapid population decline in the near future
relations.object_summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

relations.subject: Malayan whistling thrush
relations.evidence: The species has areas of undisturbed habitat, but may be threatened by habitat destruction and degradation caused by conversion of forest to agricultural land, water pollution, invasive species and overdevelopment. Because the species may have a small range and a small population, the IUCN Red List of Threatened Species has listed it as near threatened
relations.object_summary: 农药、工业污染与水体污染影响食物链安全。

relations.subject: Malayan whistling thrush
relations.evidence: The species has areas of undisturbed habitat, but may be threatened by habitat destruction and degradation caused by conversion of forest to agricultural land, water pollution, invasive species and overdevelopment. Because the species may have a small range and a small population, the IUCN Red List of Threatened Species has listed it as near threatened
relations.object_summary: 种群规模过小导致遗传多样性下降和脆弱性上升。
